attrs.alt: PIM Alerts and Access Reviews: continuous governance cycle with alert types and review lifecycle

content.text: Two Continuous Governance Mechanisms

PIM ships with two complementary mechanisms for keeping privileged access clean. PIM security alerts fire reactively when the system detects a violation — a Global Administrator count that exceeds your threshold, a role assigned directly through ARM without going through PIM, or an eligible assignment that has never been activated. Access reviews work proactively: you schedule recurring certification campaigns where designated reviewers confirm (or revoke) each eligible assignment on a cadence you control.
Neither mechanism replaces the other. Alerts stop specific misconfiguration patterns quickly; access reviews handle drift — assignments that were correctly created but are no longer needed.
PIM Alert Types

PIM evaluates alert conditions continuously and surfaces findings in the Entra admin center under Identity Governance > Privileged Identity Management > Azure AD roles or Azure resources > Alerts. Each alert has a severity and a list of affected principals or roles.
Alert
Severity
Trigger Condition
Remediation
Roles assigned outside of PIM
High
A privileged role is directly assigned (not via PIM eligible/active) to a user or group
Remove the direct assignment and recreate it as a PIM eligible assignment
Too many global administrators
High
Global Admin count exceeds configured threshold (default 5)
Reduce GA count; convert to more-scoped Entra roles where possible
Roles are being activated too frequently
Medium
A single user activates the same role more than N times in a configured window
Investigate whether the eligible assignment should be made active, or whether a task is misconfigured
Stale eligible assignments
Medium
An eligible assignment has not been activated for more than the configured threshold (default 90 days)
Review with the user: remove the assignment if no longer needed
Potential stale accounts with privileged roles
Medium
A privileged account has not signed in for 90+ days
Disable or delete the account; remove all role assignments first
Duplicate role assignments
Low
A principal holds both an eligible and an active assignment for the same role at the same scope
Remove the redundant active assignment and rely on the eligible path
Roles don’t require MFA for activation
Medium
PIM policy for a high-privilege role has MFA on activation disabled
Enable 'Require MFA on activation' in the role setting policy
All alert thresholds (admin count, activation frequency, stale days) are configurable per-tenant. Navigate to the alert row and select 'Edit' to change the threshold.
Reading and Remediating Alerts with the API
PIM alerts are accessible via the Microsoft Graph API under the unifiedRoleManagementAlert resource. This is useful for automated scanning and integration with ITSM systems.

# List all active PIM alerts for Entra (directory) roles
# Requires RoleManagement.Read.All or PrivilegedEligibilitySchedule.Read.All
$alerts = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/roleManagementAlerts/alerts?$filter=scopeType eq directoryRole'

foreach ($alert in $alerts.value) {
    Write-Host "$($alert.alertDefinitionId) - Severity: $($alert.severity) - Active: $($alert.isActive)"
}

# Get affected items for a specific alert
$alertId = 'TooManyGlobalAdminsAssignedToTenantAlert'
$items = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/roleManagementAlerts/alerts/$alertId/alertIncidents"

$items.value | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "  Affected principal: $($_.id)"
}

# Remediate (dismiss) an alert after fixing the root cause
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/roleManagementAlerts/alerts/$alertId/dismiss"
Alert Notifications

Each alert type can send email notifications to a configured list of recipients. Configure this in the PIM alert settings or via the alertConfiguration resource. Security teams typically route PIM alert emails to a shared security inbox or a ticketing system webhook.

# Read current notification configuration for an alert
$cfg = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/roleManagementAlerts/alertConfigurations/TooManyGlobalAdminsAssignedToTenantAlert'

# Update threshold and notification recipients
$body = @{
    'alertDefinitionId'     = 'TooManyGlobalAdminsAssignedToTenantAlert'
    'isEnabled'             = $true
    'scopeType'             = 'DirectoryRole'
    'scopeId'               = '/'
    'alertConfiguration'    = @{
        '@odata.type'       = '#microsoft.graph.tooManyGlobalAdminsAssignedToTenantAlertConfiguration'
        'globalAdminCountThreshold' = 4
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PATCH \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/roleManagementAlerts/alertConfigurations/TooManyGlobalAdminsAssignedToTenantAlert' \
  -Body $body -ContentType 'application/json'
Access Reviews for PIM Roles

An access review is a scheduled certification campaign. A reviewer — a manager, a resource owner, or a designated security reviewer — sees a list of all users who hold (or are eligible for) a specific role and must decide for each: 'Does this person still need this access?' Reviews can be one-time or recurring, and results can be applied automatically when the review period ends.
Creating an Access Review

Access reviews for Azure AD roles are created under Identity Governance > Access Reviews > New access review. For PIM eligible assignments, set 'Review type' to 'Teams + Groups' or 'Applications' as appropriate, then filter to the specific role.
Review Settings That Matter Most
Setting
Recommended Value
Reason
defaultDecision
Deny
If a reviewer does not respond before the period ends, the assignment is removed. Forces active participation.
autoApplyDecisionsEnabled
true
Removes denied assignments automatically at period end without a manual 'apply' step.
justificationRequiredOnApproval
true
Reviewer must provide a business reason for every approval. Creates audit evidence.
recommendationsEnabled
true
PIM suggests 'Approve' or 'Deny' based on last sign-in and activation history. Helps reviewers handle large lists.
reminderNotificationsEnabled
true
Sends reminder emails at the halfway point. Reduces missed deadlines.
instanceDurationInDays
14–21 days
Gives reviewers enough time without letting stale access persist too long.
The Reviewer Experience

Reviewers receive an email link to myaccess.microsoft.com, where they see a list of every user under review with a recommendation (a green checkmark for likely still needed, a red X for likely stale). Reviewers click Approve or Deny for each entry and type a justification.
Self-review: for low-risk roles, configure the user themselves as the reviewer. They confirm they still need the access.
Manager review: set reviewers to 'Manager of user'. Each manager sees only their direct reports. Scales well for large organizations.
Resource owner review: for Azure resource roles, the resource owner is often the best reviewer.
No reviewer assigned: if you select 'Self' and a user has no manager, they review themselves by default.

Recommendation quality depends on Entra sign-in logs. For break-glass accounts that are intentionally never used, recommendations will always suggest 'Deny'. Annotate such accounts clearly and exclude them from automated deny policies.
Monitoring Review Progress

# List all access review definitions
$defs = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions'

# Get the latest instance of a review definition and check completion
$defId = '<review-definition-id>'
$instances = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/$defId/instances"

$latest = $instances.value | Sort-Object startDateTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
Write-Host "Status: $($latest.status)  End: $($latest.endDateTime)"

# Check individual decisions in the instance
$decisions = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/$defId/instances/$($latest.id)/decisions"

foreach ($d in $decisions.value) {
    Write-Host "$($d.principal.displayName): $($d.decision) - $($d.justification)"
}
Manually Applying Review Results
When autoApplyDecisionsEnabled is false, a Privileged Role Administrator must manually apply decisions after the review period closes. This gives a final human check before access is revoked.

# Apply decisions for a completed review instance
$defId      = '<definition-id>'
$instanceId = '<instance-id>'

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/$defId/instances/$instanceId/applyDecisions"

# Export a CSV of all decisions for audit evidence
$decisions = (Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/$defId/instances/$instanceId/decisions").value

$decisions | Select-Object @{N='User';E={$_.principal.displayName}},
    @{N='Role';E={$_.resource.displayName}}, decision, justification, reviewedDateTime |
    Export-Csv -Path '~/pim-review-decisions.csv' -NoTypeInformation
Integrating Alerts and Reviews into a Governance Program
Alerts and reviews address different failure modes. A complete governance program uses both.
Failure Mode
Detected By
How
Direct ARM assignment bypasses PIM
PIM Alert
'Roles assigned outside of PIM' alert fires within minutes
Global Admin count exceeds policy
PIM Alert
'Too many global admins' alert fires immediately on violation
Role no longer needed (person changed teams)
Access Review
Quarterly review; manager denies approval for ex-team-member
Eligible assignment never used
PIM Alert + Review
Stale alert flags it; review confirms and removes it
Break-glass account incorrectly included in review scope
Review design
Exclude break-glass accounts from review scope explicitly
Credential harvested for eligible-but-never-activated role
Access Review
Remove the eligible assignment if the user hasn’t activated it in 90 days
Exporting Alert and Review Data to Azure Monitor
Route Entra diagnostic logs to a Log Analytics workspace to query both alert triggers and review decisions over time. This is the foundation for long-term access governance reporting.

# Enable Entra diagnostic settings to Log Analytics
az monitor diagnostic-settings create \
  --name pim-governance-diag \
  --resource /tenants/<tenant-id>/providers/microsoft.aad/diagnosticSettings \
  --workspace /subscriptions/<sub>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/<ws> \
  --logs '[
    {"category":"AuditLogs","enabled":true},
    {"category":"SignInLogs","enabled":true}
  ]'

// Find all PIM role activations in the last 30 days
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has 'Add member to role completed (PIM activation)'
| extend User       = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend Role       = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend Justification = tostring(AdditionalDetails[?(@.key=='reason')].value)
| project TimeGenerated, User, Role, ResultDescription, Justification
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// Find access review denials that removed eligible assignments
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where OperationName == 'Remove member from role (PIM, access review)'
| extend Reviewer = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend Subject  = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
| extend Role     = tostring(TargetResources[1].displayName)
| project TimeGenerated, Reviewer, Subject, Role
Alert Suppression and False Positives
Some alerts produce false positives in intentional configurations. Two common scenarios and how to handle them:

Break-glass accounts: These permanent Global Administrator accounts will always trigger 'Too many global admins' and 'Stale accounts with privileged roles' alerts. Increase the threshold or exclude them from the stale-account alert using the exclusion list in alert settings.

Service principals with permanent assignments: Automation service principals that need permanent Owner access will trigger 'Roles assigned outside of PIM' because PIM does not support SPN eligible assignments at all Azure role scopes. Document these exceptions and suppress the alert for those specific SPN object IDs.

Duplicate assignment alerts during migrations: When migrating from direct assignments to PIM eligible assignments, a brief window exists where both exist. Dismiss these alert instances manually during the migration window.
Access Reviews for Azure Resource Roles

Access reviews for Azure resource roles (Contributor, Owner, custom roles) differ slightly in scope from Entra role reviews. They are created under PIM > Azure Resources > [resource] > Access Reviews.
Summary
PIM security alerts detect configuration violations in real time: direct assignments that bypass PIM, too many global admins, stale eligible assignments, and roles activated outside of PIM.
Configure alert thresholds and notification recipients to match your organization's policy. Route notifications to a shared security inbox or ITSM integration.
Access reviews schedule recurring certification campaigns where reviewers confirm that eligible assignments are still needed. Set defaultDecision = 'Deny' so non-responses result in removal.
Enable autoApplyDecisionsEnabled to remove denied assignments automatically; require justifications to build an audit trail.
Both alerts and reviews surface events to the Entra audit log. Route audit logs to Log Analytics to query activation history and access review outcomes alongside other security signals.
Together, alerts handle reactive detection of violations and access reviews handle proactive recertification of drift — both are needed for a complete PIM governance program.
